rationale: Both correctly diagnose the same root cause (default --run-level=core_model forces dummy weights) and land on the same 'closed as invalid, rerun with --run-level=full_model' disposition matching the thread. Y is more thoroughly grounded, citing a wider set of specific files/lines (run_args.py, stage_config.py twice, runtime.py, assertions.py, the test's marker line) and explicitly explains the subtle point that pytest.mark.full_model is a selection marker independent of --run-level, which X doesn't address. Y also engages more completely with the reporter's actual situation (A100 vs the H100/MI325 hardware_test declaration, video path existing), while X's answer has redundant near-duplicate content between its draft_answer and additional_context sections.
